summary: Solid soundboard capture of an above‑average 1989 show with strong first‑set performances, though some listeners report occasional skips on “Man Smart (Woman Smarter)”.
review: The show is generally regarded as a solid mid‑‘80s outing – the first set rolls along with tight renditions of Cold Rain & Snow, When I Paint My Masterpiece and Deal, while the second set delivers a crisp All Along the Watchtower and a high‑energy Not Fade Away.  The Hinko SBD source is praised for its clear mix and low noise, but a few reviewers note disruptive skips or edits on “Man Smart (Woman Smarter)”.

ai_rating.rationale: Most detailed reviews rate the musical performance around B+/B‑ (≈3.5/5) and praise the soundboard recording (≈4/5).  Combining show weight (70 %) and recording weight (30 %) yields an overall rating of ~3.6.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp, low‑noise SBD capture; a few listeners report disruptive skips or patchy edits on “Man Smart (Woman Smarter)”, but no widespread distortion or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is consistently above average, highlighted by tight keyboard work and strong vocals; second set maintains the momentum with a powerful Watchtower and high‑energy close, though some post‑production edits on “Weir Here” are noted.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Great slide work on Walkin' Blues and near‑perfect Row Jimmy; overall solid lead guitar throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby stretches to his limits on Masterpiece, delivers strong vocals on When I Paint My Masterpiece, Looks Like Rain and Deal.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards are described as solid and “above‑average” for the night.
